verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein with a large extracellular ectodomain (four-helix bundle + Ig-like domain) and a short cytoplasmic tail — it has its own membrane anchor and the ectodomain is unambiguously exposed on the outer leaflet. However, expression is strictly restricted to the testis/mature sperm (a narrow germline/reproductive lineage), and surface display occurs specifically after the acrosome reaction when IZUMO1 translocates from the acrosomal membrane to the equatorial segment of the sperm plasma membrane. Because surface accessibility is confined to this narrow germline/developmental lineage (sperm), the verdict is contextual / tissue_restricted_surface rather than yes.
